decks.notes: The main concept of this UR Aggro Pioneer deck is to quickly deploy efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while utilizing spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and burn the opponent's creatures. The deck also focuses on drawing cards and looting through spells like Stock Up and Vivi Ornitier, in order to maintain card advantage and keep up the pressure on the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to be aggressive and proactive, using a mix of creatures and spells to overwhelm the opponent and secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes support cards like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse for removal, as well as Guardian of New Benalia and Monument to Endurance for additional value. The mana base is designed to support the combo pieces, with cards like Eiganjo and Takenuma for utility. Overall, the deck aims to control the board while setting up the combo for a decisive victory.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on synergies between sacrificing creatures, dealing damage, and generating value. Cards like Witch's Oven and Mayhem Devil work together to ping opponents and control the board. Bloodtithe Harvester and Scavenger's Talent provide more sacrifice options, while Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and The Meathook Massacre offer powerful finishers. The deck aims to control the board early on with removal spells like Fatal Push and then overwhelm opponents with a combination of damage and sacrifice effects to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly getting creatures onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. By utilizing cards like Collected Company to fetch creatures from the deck and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to disrupt the opponent's spells, this Selesnya Aggro deck aims to control the pace of the game and maintain board presence. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Archon of Emeria further solidifies its strategy of applying pressure and forcing the opponent to react defensively in order to survive.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combo between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate immense value and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards such as Guardian of New Benalia and Sheltered by Ghosts to protect the combo pieces and maintain board presence. It also features disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's strategy. The mana base is carefully constructed with lands like Godless Shrine and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to support the combo and ensure consistent performance.

The main concept of this Gruul Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By utilizing cards like Questing Druid, Emberheart Challenger, Monastery Swiftspear, and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to quickly chip away at the opponent's life total. The inclusion of creatures with prowess and abilities that trigger on spells being cast, as well as removal spells like Academic Dispute, helps maintain the pressure on the opponent and create advantageous board states.

The main concept behind this Red Deck Wins aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent. This is achieved through a mix of creatures with haste, direct damage spells, and enchantments that boost damage output. The deck aims to control the board early on and maintain pressure throughout the game, utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage along with burn spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning. By maintaining a fast pace and overwhelming the opponent with consistent damage, the deck seeks to secure a victory before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a combination of efficient removal spells and hand disruption cards to disrupt the opponent's game plan. By playing cards like Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Abrupt Decay, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain board control. In addition, the deck also includes powerful creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to pressure the opponent and close out the game. The selection of lands and mana dorks allows for consistent mana development to support the control elements of the deck.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on using cheap spells and creatures with prowess abilities to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By casting spells like Fiery Impulse and Boomerang Basics, along with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck aims to build a board presence and apply pressure early on in the game. By utilizing cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent, the deck can also disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining a fast-paced aggressive game plan. Land cards like Den of the Bugbear and Riverpyre Verge provide mana fixing and additional threats to keep the opponent on their toes.

The deck focuses on using low-cost aggressive creatures like Emberheart Challenger, Soul-Scar Mage, and Monastery Swiftspear to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board while maintaining pressure. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures, backed up by a strong removal package. It also utilizes cards like Academic Dispute and Heroes' Hangout to draw cards and maintain card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to win the game through consistent damage output and efficient play.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a combo using Greasefang and Parhelion II, supported by cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board. The deck also features various lands and creatures to support the combo, with options for removal and card draw to maintain consistency and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to efficiently assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful synergy between Greasefang, Parhelion II, and supporting cards.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize for control, while utilizing Monument to Endurance and Iron-Shield Elf for protection. The combo pieces, Greasefang and Parhelion II, are key to the strategy and can quickly turn the tide of the game. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Guardian of New Benalia and Fleeting Spirit for additional support and consistency. Overall, the goal is to efficiently set up the combo and dominate the battlefield.

The deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing low-cost creatures and pump spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Callous Sell-Sword, backed up by pump spells like Titan's Strength and Monstrous Rage to increase their power and close out the game swiftly. The inclusion of cards like Slickshot Show-Off and Leyline of Resonance adds additional pressure and disruption to the opponent's game plan, making it difficult for them to stabilize and recover.

The deck focuses on using Lotus Field as a key component for generating mana quickly and efficiently to fuel powerful combos. By utilizing cards like Scapeshift, Lumra, Bellow of the Woods, and Arboreal Grazer, the deck aims to ramp up mana production and eventually win the game through explosive plays. The deck also includes cards like Spelunking, The Wandering Minstrel, and Aftermath Analyst to dig for key pieces and maintain card advantage. Additionally, cards like Port of Karfell and Boseiju, Who Endures provide utility and protection for the combo pieces.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively overwhelm the opponent with low-cost spells and creatures. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and finish off the opponent with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Riverglide Pathway and Stormchaser's Talent to help maintain card advantage and keep up the tempo. The deck focuses on fast-paced gameplay and efficient removal to outpace the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells, removal, and card draw. It aims to outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats and establishing a solid defense. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional synergy and value. The deck utilizes a variety of lands to fix mana and provide utility. Overall, the key concept of this UW Control deck is to control the game tempo, draw cards, and win through incremental advantage.
